scope: Pass B full-surface expansion, verified against the live, versioned API Blueprint source files that generate developers.sparkpost.com (fetched 2026-07-03 from github.com/SparkPost/developers.sparkpost.com/content/api/*.apib -- the authoritative machine-readable source, more complete/precise than the rendered HTML). 15 read streams (5 legacy-parity: recipient_lists/templates/sending_domains/transmissions/suppression_list, plus 10 newly added: ip_pools/webhooks/subaccounts/tracking_domains/inbound_domains/relay_webhooks/sending_ips/ab_tests/account) and 25 write actions (update_account, transmission send, recipient-list/template/sending-domain/suppression/ip-pool/webhook/subaccount/tracking-domain/inbound-domain/relay-webhook lifecycle CRUD) are covered_by a stream or write. Every request body checked this pass is FLAT (no Rails-style root-key wrapping, unlike solarwinds-service-desk in this same wave) -- ordinary body_type:'json' write actions apply cleanly. Excluded categories: deprecated (bounce-domains and message-events are explicitly, currently marked discontinued/deprecated by their own .apib content, in favor of sending-domains and events respectively), out_of_scope (the ~30-endpoint deliverability-metrics/analytics-aggregation matrix, raw event-log search/ingest endpoints whose only pagination mechanism is a RELATIVE next-page URL this dialect's next_url paginator cannot follow -- see below -- seed-list/inline-seeds sandbox-testing tooling, snippets under the separate /labs prefix, and several niche sub-resource/verification/preview actions), duplicate_of (single-record-by-id GETs already covered by a list stream), non_data_endpoint (usage/documentation/sample introspection), requires_elevated_scope (recipient validation and data-privacy requests, both entailing real external side effects on third-party data with no natural place in an ordinary read/write sync model), binary_payload (n/a this pass -- no binary endpoints found).

excluded.reason: creates a running A/B test with a full variant/audience-selection body; breadth-vs-cost triage for this pass -- the ab_tests stream covers the read side

submits a legal right-to-be-forgotten data-deletion request affecting real recipient PII across the account; a compliance/legal action outside the scope of an ordinary connector write, requiring dedicated authorization/process, not a routine sync mutation
submits a third-party-data opt-out compliance request; same reasoning as rtbf-request above
bulk-ingests externally-sourced (non-SparkPost) event data into SparkPost's own analytics pipeline; a specialized data-ingestion action, not a plain object mutation this pass models

ENGINE_GAP-adjacent: raw message-event search/pagination. This endpoint's own pagination is cursor-based, but the cursor value is embedded ONLY inside a RELATIVE next-page URL in the response body's links.next field (e.g. "/api/v1/events/message?...&cursor=...", confirmed from events.apib's own documented examples) -- this dialect's next_url paginator (paginate.go's checkOrigin) fails closed on any URL with no host ("has no host; rejecting"), so a relative next_url can never be followed, and the cursor pagination type (token_path) has no path-parsing filter to extract a bare cursor value out of an embedded URL string. Combined with the endpoint's huge, largely-required filter-parameter surface (30+ query params) and high-volume raw-log shape, this is excluded as a breadth-vs-cost + partial-ENGINE_GAP call rather than shipping a single-page-only implementation of what is fundamentally a paginated, high-cardinality event log.
search over ingested (non-SparkPost-native) event data; same relative-next-url pagination gap as events/message, and its own out-of-scope ingest data source
